description_long: This is a carved stone block featuring intricate reliefs and decorative motifs on its vertical faces. One side displays a circular pattern of swirling spirals, while two other sides are adorned with animal figures and possibly symbolic or narrative scenes. The stone appears weathered, with surface pitting and erosion, suggesting significant age and exposure to the elements. Adjacent to the main block is a smaller, bowl-like stone with a hollowed center, possibly used as a basin or offering receptacle. The overall form and ornamentation suggest it may have served a ceremonial or boundary-marking function in an urban or park setting.
description: A weathered, carved stone block with animal reliefs and spiral motifs, accompanied by a smaller hollowed stone basin.
description_view_0: Front view shows a rectangular stone block with animal relief carvings and a smaller, round, hollowed stone at its base.
description_view_1: Side view highlights a single animal relief and the depth of the stone block, with the basin visible beside it.
description_view_2: Rear view features a prominent circular spiral motif carved into the stone, with the basin seen from above.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays additional animal reliefs and the hollowed basin from a different angle.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on a paved or landscaped area
attribute_pattern: Features artificial carvings including swirling spirals and animal reliefs, with regular, deliberate patterns on multiple faces.
attribute_weathering: Rough and pitted with visible erosion, indicating long-term exposure to weather and possibly human contact.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with modern urban elements but may be partially surrounded by paving stones or low vegetation, serving as a focal point or boundary marker.
